title: Neutral Triplet Sum
description:
Return all unique triplets [a,b,c] with a + b + c = 0. The solution should avoid duplicates and be better than O(n^3).

    

Constraints:
    - 3 ≤ len(nums) ≤ 10^4
- -10^4 ≤ nums[i] ≤ 10^4
explanation:
Intuition
Sorting allows a for loop to fix the first value and a two pointer sweep to find complements. Skipping equal values prevents producing duplicate triplets.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Sort the array.
2. Main Algorithm: For each i, two pointer scan for pairs that sum to -a while skipping equal values on all pointers.
3. Return Result: Collect each found triplet once and continue scanning.

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n^2) because the scan adjusts pointers a limited
  number of times and avoids nested loops.
- Space Complexity: O(1) since only a few variables or an output
  list are used.

Key Insights
- Sorting turns triplet search into many two sum scans
- Skipping duplicates avoids redundant answers

Common Pitfalls
- Forgetting to skip duplicates creates repeated triplets
- Using sets can be slower and more complex
hints: Sort first to handle duplicates cleanly
Fix one index and sweep the rest with two pointers
Skip equal values after finding a triplet
